---
title: "Module 4 Assignment 3"
output: word_document
date: "2026-07-14"
---

```{r}
library(tidymodels)
library(tidyverse)
library(caret)
library(vip)
library(gridExtra)
library(ranger)
```

## R Markdown



```{r}
drug = read_csv("drug_data-2.csv")
```

## Including Plots



```{r}
names(drug) = c("ID", "Age", "Gender", "Education", "Country", "Ethnicity",
"Nscore", "Escore", "Oscore", "Ascore", "Cscore", "Impulsive",
"SS", "Alcohol", "Amphet", "Amyl", "Benzos", "Caff", "Cannabis",
"Choc", "Coke", "Crack", "Ecstasy", "Heroin", "Ketamine", "Legalh",
"LSD", "Meth", "Mushrooms", "Nicotine", "Semer", "VSA")
```

```{r}
drug[drug == "CL0"] = "No"
drug[drug == "CL1"] = "No"
drug[drug == "CL2"] = "Yes"
drug[drug == "CL3"] = "Yes"
drug[drug == "CL4"] = "Yes"
drug[drug == "CL5"] = "Yes"
drug[drug == "CL6"] = "Yes"
```

```{r}
drug_clean = drug %>% mutate_at(vars(Age:Ethnicity), funs(as_factor)) %>%
mutate(Age = factor(Age, labels = c("18_24", "25_34", "35_44", "45_54",
"55_64", "65_"))) %>%
mutate(Gender = factor(Gender, labels = c("Male", "Female"))) %>%
mutate(Education = factor(Education, labels = c("Under16", "At16", "At17", "At18",
"SomeCollege","ProfessionalCert",
"Bachelors", "Masters",
"Doctorate"))) %>%
mutate(Country = factor(Country, labels = c("USA", "NewZealand", "Other", "Australia",
"Ireland","Canada","UK"))) %>%
mutate(Ethnicity = factor(Ethnicity, labels = c("Black", "Asian", "White",
"White/Black", "Other",
"White/Asian", "Black/Asian"))) %>%
mutate_at(vars(Alcohol:VSA), funs(as_factor)) %>%
select(-ID)
```


```{r}
str(drug_clean)
```

```{r}
drug_clean = drug_clean %>% select(!(Alcohol:Mushrooms)) %>% select(!(Semer:VSA))
```

```{r}
library(naniar)
gg_miss_var(drug_clean)
```
```{r}
set.seed(1234) 
drug_clean_split = initial_split(drug_clean, prop = 0.7, strata = Nicotine) #70% in training
train = training(drug_clean_split)
test = testing(drug_clean_split)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# --- Group 1: Demographics (Categorical) ---
p1 <- ggplot(drug_clean, aes(x = Age, fill = Nicotine)) + 
  geom_bar(position = "fill") + 
  labs(y = "Proportion", title = "Nicotine by Age") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

p2 <- ggplot(drug_clean, aes(x = Gender, fill = Nicotine)) + 
  geom_bar(position = "fill") + 
  labs(y = "Proportion", title = "Nicotine by Gender")

p3 <- ggplot(drug_clean, aes(x = Education, fill = Nicotine)) + 
  geom_bar(position = "fill") + 
  labs(y = "Proportion", title = "Nicotine by Education") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

p4 <- ggplot(drug_clean, aes(x = Country, fill = Nicotine)) + 
  geom_bar(position = "fill") + 
  labs(y = "Proportion", title = "Nicotine by Country")


# --- Group 2: Ethnicity & Personality Scores (N, E, O) ---
p5 <- ggplot(drug_clean, aes(x = Ethnicity, fill = Nicotine)) + 
  geom_bar(position = "fill") + 
  labs(y = "Proportion", title = "Nicotine by Ethnicity") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

p6 <- ggplot(drug_clean, aes(x = Nicotine, y = Nscore, fill = Nicotine)) + 
  geom_boxplot() + 
  labs(title = "Neurotism (Nscore)")

p7 <- ggplot(drug_clean, aes(x = Nicotine, y = Escore, fill = Nicotine)) + 
  geom_boxplot() + 
  labs(title = "Extraversion (Escore)")

p8 <- ggplot(drug_clean, aes(x = Nicotine, y = Oscore, fill = Nicotine)) + 
  geom_boxplot() + 
  labs(title = "Openness (Oscore)")


# --- Group 3: Personality Scores (A, C, Impulsive, SS) ---
p9 <- ggplot(drug_clean, aes(x = Nicotine, y = Ascore, fill = Nicotine)) + 
  geom_boxplot() + 
  labs(title = "Agreeableness (Ascore)")

p10 <- ggplot(drug_clean, aes(x = Nicotine, y = Cscore, fill = Nicotine)) + 
  geom_boxplot() + 
  labs(title = "Conscientiousness (Cscore)")

p11 <- ggplot(drug_clean, aes(x = Nicotine, y = Impulsive, fill = Nicotine)) + 
  geom_boxplot() + 
  labs(title = "Impulsivity")

p12 <- ggplot(drug_clean, aes(x = Nicotine, y = SS, fill = Nicotine)) + 
  geom_boxplot() + 
  labs(title = "Sensation Seeking")
# Display the first 4 plots (Demographics)
grid.arrange(p1, p2, p3, p4, ncol = 2, nrow = 2)

# Display the next 4 plots
grid.arrange(p5, p6, p7, p8, ncol = 2, nrow = 2)

# Display the final 4 plots
grid.arrange(p9, p10, p11, p12, ncol = 2, nrow = 2)
```

```{r}

# Ensure tidymodels is set to use the ranger engine
# (Note: ranger handles variable importance if needed later)

# --- 1. Define the Recipe ---
# Predict Nicotine using all other variables in drug_clean
rf_recipe <- recipe(Nicotine ~ ., data = train)

# --- 2. Model Specification ---
# Allow mtry and min_n to be tuned, set trees to 100, and use the ranger engine
rf_spec <- rand_forest(
  mtry = tune(),
  min_n = tune(),
  trees = 100
) %>% 
  set_engine("ranger", importance = "permutation") %>% 
  set_mode("classification")

# --- 3. Create the Workflow ---
rf_workflow <- workflow() %>% 
  add_recipe(rf_recipe) %>% 
  add_model(rf_spec)

# --- 4. Set up 5-Fold Cross-Validation ---
# Set the fold seed to 123 as instructed
set.seed(123)
rf_folds <- vfold_cv(train, v = 5, strata = Nicotine)

# --- 5. Define the Tuning Grid ---
# Allow mtry between 2 and 8, min_n between 5 and 20, with 10 levels
rf_grid <- grid_regular(
  mtry(range = c(2, 8)),
  min_n(range = c(5, 20)),
  levels = 10
)

# --- 6. Tune the Grid ---
# Set the tuning seed to 123 as instructed
set.seed(123)
rf_tuning_results <- tune_grid(
  rf_workflow,
  resamples = rf_folds,
  grid = rf_grid,
  metrics = metric_set(accuracy) # We focus on accuracy for the question
)

# --- 7. Visualize the Tuning Performance ---
# This will generate the plot to help you find the highest accuracy value
autoplot(rf_tuning_results)
```


```{r}
# --- 1. Select the Best Parameters ---
# This automatically extracts the mtry and min_n that gave the highest accuracy
best_rf_params <- select_best(rf_tuning_results, metric = "accuracy")

# --- 2. Finalize the Workflow ---
# This plugs the best mtry and min_n values back into your workflow
final_rf_workflow <- finalize_workflow(rf_workflow, best_rf_params)

# --- 3. Fit the Final Model to the Training Set ---
# We fit this finalized workflow to our training data
final_rf_fit <- fit(final_rf_workflow, data = train)

# --- 4. Extract and Plot Variable Importance ---
# We pull the underlying engine fit (ranger) to extract the importance scores

final_rf_fit %>%
  extract_fit_parsnip() %>%
  vip(num_features = 10) # Displays the top 10 most important variables
```

```{r}
# 1. Generate predictions on the training set
train_preds <- predict(final_rf_fit, new_data = train) %>%
  bind_cols(train)

# 2. Calculate accuracy
train_preds %>% 
  accuracy(truth = Nicotine, estimate = .pred_class)
```


```{r}
# Calculate the proportions of "Yes" and "No" in the training set
train %>%
  count(Nicotine) %>%
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n))
```
```{r}
# 1. Generate predictions on the testing set
test_preds <- predict(final_rf_fit, new_data = test) %>%
  bind_cols(test)

# 2. Calculate accuracy
test_preds %>% 
  accuracy(truth = Nicotine, estimate = .pred_class)
```
